facts.text: Japan is an island nation in East Asia made up of four main islands: Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu, and Shikoku.
Tokyo is Japan's capital and one of the largest metropolitan areas in the world.
Mount Fuji is Japan's most famous mountain and an enduring national symbol.
Japan is known for high-speed Shinkansen bullet trains, which are celebrated for speed, efficiency, and punctuality.
Japanese cuisine includes sushi, ramen, tempura, udon, soba, and many region-specific specialties.
Cherry blossom season, known as sakura, is one of Japan's most famous annual cultural events.
Japan uses three writing systems together: kanji, hiragana, and katakana.
The country has a long history of emperors, shoguns, samurai culture, and rapid modernization during the Meiji era.
Japan hosted the Summer Olympics in Tokyo in 1964 and again in 2021 after the pandemic delay.
Anime, manga, video games, and technology exports have made Japan a major cultural influence worldwide.
Shinto and Buddhism have both deeply shaped Japanese religious and cultural life.
Japan sits on the Pacific Ring of Fire, so earthquakes and volcanic activity are important parts of its geography.
Historic cities like Kyoto and Nara preserve many temples, shrines, gardens, and wooden architecture from earlier eras.
Japan combines deep historical tradition with some of the world's most advanced urban technology and design.
Mount Fuji is Japan's most famous peak and a long-standing national symbol.
Its near-perfect volcanic shape made it one of the most recognizable mountains in the world.
Mount Fuji is one of the most recognizable places or topics associated with Japan.
Travel guides about Japan frequently feature Mount Fuji.
Learning about Mount Fuji helps explain part of the geography, culture, or history of Japan.
For many visitors, Mount Fuji stands out as a major highlight of Japan.
Maps, documentaries, and guidebooks about Japan often highlight Mount Fuji.
The story of Mount Fuji is closely tied to how many people understand Japan.
People around the world often associate Japan with places or traditions like Mount Fuji.
Mount Fuji is frequently mentioned when describing the diversity and appeal of Japan.
One reason Japan feels so memorable to travelers is the presence of places such as Mount Fuji.
From tourism to history, Mount Fuji plays a notable role in how Japan is presented to the world.
